 {"id":"gt-aqm","title":"Beads as Universal Data Plane","description":"## Vision

Beads is the data plane for all Gas Town operations. Everything flows through beads:
- Work items (issues, tasks, epics)
- Mail (messages between agents)
- Merge requests (queue entries)
- Workflows (composable execution patterns)
- Resources (leases, locks, quotas)
- Schedules (timed activities)

## New Bead Categories

### Molecules (Composable Workflows)
Crystallized workflow patterns that can be attached to work items.

### Timed Beads (Scheduled Work)
Beads that wake up periodically via daemon.

### Pinned Beads (Ongoing Concerns)
Beads representing persistent concerns, not discrete tasks.

### Resource Beads (Leases/Locks)
Beads representing reserved resources.

## v1 Priority

- **P0**: Molecules (enables engineer-in-box)
- **P2**: Timed, Pinned, Resource beads (post-v1)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:05:57.847578-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:10.898712-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:29.881371-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-caz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:19.833603-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-8h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:19.930166-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-aqm","depends_on_id":"gt-b3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:20.026188-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-b1g","title":"MVP Cutover: GGT replaces PGT for batch work","description":"When this is closed, stop using town and start using gt.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. gt spawn assigns issue to polecat and starts session
2. gt spawn --epic spawns workers for all epic children
3. gt session manages tmux lifecycle  
4. gt send / gt inbox work for mail
5. Refinery processes merge queue with semantic merges
6. Integration branches created and landed correctly
7. gt stop --all halts all sessions
8. One successful test batch completed end-to-end

## What Must Work

- Spawn polecat with issue assignment
- Spawn workers for epic children
- Session start/stop/attach
- Mail send/inbox/read
- Refinery merge loop (semantic)
- Integration branch → main landing
- Witness cleanup protocol
- Emergency stop

## What Can Be Deferred

- Doctor checks (use PGT)
- TUI dashboard
- Plugin system
- Federation
- Ephemeral rigs
- Detailed landing reports

## Test Plan

1. Create epic with 2 tasks, spawn 2 workers
2. Verify polecats get assigned and sessions start
3. Simulate task completion
4. Verify Refinery merges to integration
5. Verify landing to main
6. Verify cleanup

## Validation

Run one real batch implementing GGT issues using GGT.

## Note

No "swarm IDs" - just spawn workers for epic, let merge queue coordinate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:09.148751-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:30.970314-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:30.970314-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.196292-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.273483-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.351097-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.431641-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.511124-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.609649-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-rm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.69062-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:32.942855-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:35.053559-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-b2hj","title":"gt orphans: Find lost polecat work","description":"Add a command to find orphaned commits that were never merged to main.

## Problem
Polecat work can get lost when:
- Session killed before merge
- Refinery fails to process
- Network issues during push

## Solution
Add `gt orphans` command that:
1. Uses `git fsck --unreachable` to find dangling commits
2. Filters to recent commits (default: 7 days, configurable)
3. Excludes stash/index entries (WIP on, index on)
4. Shows commit details and suggests recovery

## Usage
```
gt orphans              # Last 7 days
gt orphans --days=14    # Last 2 weeks
gt orphans --recover    # Interactive cherry-pick
```

## Found orphans (Dec 16-20, 2025)
- 3b146c11: Fix mail read auto-ack and add Mayor startup directive
- b6fdc561: Add persistent theme config and fix crew session theming
- 97aba756: Unify gt prime to call bd prime and mail check
- ce769ca5: Add mol-bootstrap molecule for Gas Town installation
- b2219de7: Add bootstrap documentation for new Gas Town installations
- bc82348: feat: Add gt done command (already recovered)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T16:27:25.584819-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T16:27:25.584819-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-w9o","title":"/restart: Personal slash command for in-place agent restart","description":"Create ~/.claude/commands/restart.md that restarts current Gas Town agent in place.

## Detection
- Read tmux session name: gt-mayor, gt-witness-*, gt-refinery-*, gt-polecat-*
- Fallback: check GT_ROLE env var

## Behavior by role
- mayor: gt mayor restart (sends Ctrl-C, loop respawns)
- witness: gt witness restart
- refinery: gt refinery restart  
- polecat: gt polecat restart (or witness-mediated)

## Command format
Uses backticks for inline bash to detect context, then instructs Claude to run appropriate restart.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:30.043125-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:43:17.182303-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T18:43:17.182303-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-wpg","title":"Replaceable notifications via Claude Code queue","description":"Leverage Claude Code's ability to replace queued text for notifications that supersede previous ones.

## Problem

If daemon sends 10 heartbeats while agent is busy, agent returns to see 10 stacked messages. Wasteful and noisy.

## Solution

Use Claude Code's queue replacement for:
- Heartbeat messages (only latest matters)
- Status updates that supersede previous
- Progress notifications

## Implementation

Notifications get a 'slot' identifier. New notification in same slot replaces old one:
- Slot: 'heartbeat' → only one heartbeat queued at a time
- Slot: 'status-<rig>' → latest status per rig
- No slot → stacks normally (for unique messages)

## Research Needed

- How does Claude Code expose queue replacement?
- tmux send-keys behavior with pending input
- Alternative: clear + resend pattern","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:29.821949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:19:00.398942-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:19:00.398942-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-wpg","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.656972-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-wusk","title":"Layered context onboarding pattern","description":"Pattern from handoff discussion:

## Pattern: Layered Context Onboarding

Town CLAUDE.md (user/org) -> Rig CLAUDE.md (project) -> Role priming

## Ultra-compressed HOP for workers (no reveal)

- Permanent record: All work tracked. Outcomes matter.
- Quality gates: Molecule steps exist for a reason.
- Attribution: Completions build your track record.
- Handoff clean: Leave state any worker can continue.

## Recommendation

Create Town @AGENTS.md for shared worker context that all workers see.
This provides common behavioral guidance without revealing full HOP context.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:55:11.984103-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T00:55:11.984103-08:00"}
